# Microsoft Sentinel

## RSA 2026
- **Booth number and hall:** N-5744
- **Any updated booth info from the conference floor:** Microsoft is hosting a "Microsoft Pre-Day" and will feature demos, theater sessions, and opportunities for conversations with Sentinel experts at their booth.

## Company Profile
- **Employee count (approximate):** Microsoft, the parent company of Sentinel, has approximately 228,000 employees as of 2026.
- **Founded year:** Microsoft was founded on April 4, 1975.
- **HQ location:** Redmond, Washington, USA.
- **Funding stage or public status / ARR if known:** Microsoft is a publicly traded company.
- **Website URL:** www.microsoft.com

## Technology Stack
- **Core technology and architecture:** Microsoft Sentinel is a cloud-native Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) and Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response (SOAR) solution built on Azure. Its architecture is designed for real-time security insights and incident management, featuring a unified security data lake, graph-powered context, and an intelligent Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. It collects logs and data from various sources using data connectors, ingesting them into a Log Analytics Workspace.
- **AI/ML frameworks or capabilities used:** Sentinel leverages AI and machine learning for AI-driven threat detection rules, which continuously evaluate incoming data to identify potential threats and adapt to new attack patterns and global threat intelligence. It provides AI-powered investigation tools to simplify threat exploration and resolution, automate repetitive tasks, construct incident timelines, and visualize attack vectors. It also incorporates User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA) and integrates generative AI.
- **Cloud infrastructure and key integrations:** Microsoft Sentinel operates on Azure infrastructure. It offers extensive integration capabilities, including out-of-the-box connectors for Microsoft services like Microsoft 365, Microsoft Defender, Azure AD, and Office 365. It also supports integration with third-party cloud providers such as AWS and Google Cloud, as well as over 350 third-party tools and custom integrations. Automated responses are often orchestrated using Azure Logic Apps.
- **Any proprietary models or data sources:** Sentinel utilizes Microsoft's proprietary threat intelligence. The Model Context Protocol (MCP) server is a key component that provides a unified, hosted interface for interacting with security data using natural language and building intelligent security agents.

## Key Customers
- **Notable customer logos or named references:** HSBC, Kao, Peraton, UBS USA, New Zealand Department of Internal Affairs, De Heus Vietnam, Spotify, Best Buy, AVEVA, St.
